title: Piquant Crispy Peruvian Udon Noodles Ceviche Clasico
description: A salty and crispy Peruvian dish featuring udon noodles and double-rise-bread, sugar-free-friendly, in the form of ceviche clasico, perfect for a monsoon day.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the double-rise-bread into 1-inch cubes and bake for 10 minutes, or until crispy.
Cook the udon noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the fresh seafood mix, lime juice, peruvian aji amarillo peppers, red onion, cilantro, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Marinate the seafood mixture in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
In a large serving dish, combine the cooked udon noodles, crispy bread cubes, and marinated seafood mixture.
Toss gently to combine and serve immediately.
Garnish with additional cilantro and lime wedges, if desired.
Serve with a side of your favorite Peruvian sauce, such as aji amarillo or huancaina.
